Elder_Health_Idea_Generation_SOP.docx.content:
Section 1: What the Numbers Say
Before generating a single idea, understand what the data says. These numbers come from 109 real videos across 6 competitor channels. Every idea must be rooted in these patterns.
1.1 Market Snapshot
1.2 The 6 Competitor Channels - Ranked by Efficiency
Efficiency = average views per video. This tells you whose formula is most worth studying.
1.3 Content Category Performance
1.4 Title Pattern Performance
Section 2: The 8 Content Pillars
Every viral video in this niche lives in one of these 8 pillars. Never generate an idea outside them. Each pillar has proven demand, working title formulas, and a data-backed view range. When you sit down to generate ideas - pick a pillar first, then build the idea inside it.
2.1 Pillar Overview
2.2 Pillar Deep-Dives
PILLAR 1: FOOD & DRINK REVELATION
Highest total views (14.7M). Model channel: Senior Facts Daily (651K avg). Formula: take ONE food or drink the audience already knows, then reveal a hidden danger OR shocking benefit. The simpler the food, the better.
"Eat 1 [FOOD] Daily - Watch What Happens to Your Body" (2.1M proven)
"Drink This [DRINK] Before Breakfast - Insane Benefits" (2.9M proven)
"Never Eat [FOOD] Like This! 5 Dangerous Mistakes" (648K proven)
"Soak [FOOD] in [LIQUID] Overnight - 5 Powerful Benefits" (497K proven)
"1 Spoon of [FOOD] Before Bed - Your Body Will Thank You" (500K proven)
PILLAR 2: EXERCISE BREAKTHROUGH
13.1M total, 375K avg. Model: Senior Wellness. Key insight: seniors want movement that fits their life with a 'better than X' hook. Cultural authority framing (Japanese, Greek) = instant credibility.
"Over [AGE]? [X] Movements [Famous Group] Elders Do Every Day" (2.5M proven)
"Over [AGE]? This Exercise Is Better Than Walking" (1M+ proven)
"This 2-Minute Exercise Relaxes the Muscle That [PROBLEM]" (1M proven)
"30-Second Test + 3 Fast Fix Exercises for [PROBLEM]" (1.2M proven)
"NEVER Do This Exercise After 60 - Do THIS Instead"
PILLAR 3: SLEEP / NIGHT PEEING
34 videos, 8.9M views - and demand is NOT saturated. Waking up 2-4x per night is the #1 pain point for the 60+ audience. Every new angle on this topic still works.
"Stop [PROBLEM] Tonight! (3-Minute Hack)" (1.5M proven)
"Stop Blaming [COMMON THING]: THIS Is Why You Are Waking Up at 2 AM" (848K proven)
"Waking Up at 2 AM? It Is NOT Old Age - It Is [X] [CAUSE]" (rising)
"[X] Foods for Deep, Uninterrupted Sleep" (141K and growing)
"This [X]-Minute Bed Routine Stops Frequent Bathroom Trips" (257K proven)
PILLAR 4: LEG & CIRCULATION
Only 6 competitor videos despite 368K average views. This is the MOST UNDERSERVED high-value pillar. Wide open for your channel to dominate.
"[X]-Second Test to Check If Your Legs Are Getting Enough Blood"
"Drink THIS 1 Cup - Your Legs Are Starving for Blood (Over 70)"
"5 Signs Your Leg Circulation Is Dangerously Low"
"Over 70? Do THIS for 3 Minutes Every Morning to Wake Up Your Legs"
"5 Drinks That Open Your Leg Arteries Faster Than Any Medication"
PILLAR 5: MUSCLE / STRENGTH
8 videos, 376K avg - second-highest average per video. Dramatically underserved. The fear of sarcopenia (muscle loss after 60) is one of the deepest anxieties in this audience.
"MUSCLES Are Disappearing Without THIS One Food" (535K proven)
"Over 60? You Are Losing 1% of Your Muscle Every Year Unless You Do This"
"5 Foods That Are Destroying Your Muscles After 60" (152K proven)
"STOP Taking This Protein Supplement After 60 - Your Body Cannot Use It"
"5 Signs You Have Dangerous Muscle Loss (And the 1-Week Fix)"
PILLAR 6: PAIN & JOINTS
7 videos, 344K avg. Knee pain, sciatica, back stiffness - universal for the 60+ audience. High demand, underserved content.
"Do THIS Every Morning - Knee Pain Starts Fading (Fast)" (716K proven)
"The ONLY [X] Exercises You Need for [PAIN] - Stop Stretching" (635K proven)
"Why You Wake Up Stiff and Sore - And the Easy Fix" (888K proven)
"The Real Reason Your Knees Hurt After 60 (Not What You Think)"
"NEVER Take Ibuprofen for Joint Pain After 60 - A Surgeon Explains"
PILLAR 7: WARNING / NEVER DO X
36 videos, 262K avg. This is a framing OVERLAY more than a standalone pillar - any topic from pillars 1-6 can be reframed as a warning. The word NEVER is the single highest-CTR word in this niche.
"7 [EVERYDAY THINGS] That Are [SECRETLY HARMING YOU]" (844K proven)
"[X] Habits KILLING Seniors Faster Than Smoking" (156K proven)
"NEVER [COMMON ACTION] After 60 - Here Is What To Do Instead"
"Be Careful: These [X] [TRUSTED THINGS] Increase Risk of [FEARED OUTCOME]"
PILLAR 8: BRAIN / MEMORY / CANCER
Lower in main database but explosively proven in the ideas database: 9 WORST Foods that Feed CANCER Cells = 1.8M, These 4 Vegetables Feed CANCER Cells = 1.3M, These 6 Foods Burn Fat and Heal Body = 2.6M. Authority framing (Nobel Prize Doctor, Japanese Doctor) is the unlock.
"[Famous Doctor / Nationality] Reveals: These [X] Foods Kill Cancer"
"[X] Foods Quietly Destroying Your Memory After 60"
"Nobel Prize Doctor: Eat These Every Day and Live to 100"
"[X] Common Drinks That Are Slowly Shrinking Your Brain"
"Brain Doctor: These [X] Foods Make Your Brain Younger in 2 Months"
Section 3: The 5-Dimension Scoring System
Never develop an idea without scoring it first. This system is calibrated to the actual performance data from 109 videos. Any idea scoring below 14 should be reworked or scrapped. Scoring takes 2 minutes and saves hours of wasted script work.
3.1 The 5 Dimensions (4 points each, 20 total)
Section 4: The 5-Step Generation Process
This is the exact sequence to follow every time you generate ideas. Do not skip steps. Do not reorder them. One session using this process produces 10-15 scored ideas in 30-45 minutes.
Section 5: The 4-Part Idea Brief Template
Every idea must be documented in this format before going to script. An undocumented idea is just a thought. This template makes it an actionable brief that can be handed to the script writer.
Section 6: 30 Ready Ideas Across All 8 Pillars
These 30 ideas have been generated using the system above, scored using the 5-dimension framework, and verified against competitor coverage. All score 15 or above. Develop them using the Script Writing SOP.
PILLAR 1: FOOD AND DRINK REVELATION
PILLAR 2: EXERCISE BREAKTHROUGH
PILLAR 3: SLEEP AND NIGHT PEEING
PILLAR 4: LEG AND CIRCULATION
PILLAR 5: MUSCLE AND STRENGTH
PILLAR 6: PAIN AND JOINTS
PILLAR 7: WARNING / NEVER DO X
PILLAR 8: BRAIN / MEMORY / CANCER
Section 7: Idea Generation Rules

Project_Upload_Guide_and_Activation_Prom.content:
The Full File List (19 Files Total)
Your Claude Project needs 19 files to operate at full power. You already have 15 of them. You need to create 4 short documents. Here is every file, what it is, and how to get it.
GROUP 1: The 9 SOPs (You Have All of These)
GROUP 2: Competitor Data (You Have Both)
GROUP 3: Competitor Transcripts (You Have All 35)
Upload all 35 transcript CSV files as a single batch. These teach Claude the exact language patterns, phrasing, analogies, and emotional beats used in the highest-performing videos in your niche.
GROUP 4: The 4 Documents You Need to Create (30 Minutes Total)
These 4 documents do not exist yet. Each one takes 5-10 minutes to create. They are what make the Claude Project generate content that is specifically yours — not generic elder health content.
The Master Upload Checklist
Step 1: The Project Instruction (Pinned in Project Settings)
Before you run any prompt, go to your Claude Project settings and paste this as the Project Instructions. This runs silently before every single conversation in the project.
Step 2: The Master Activation Prompt (Run This First)
After uploading all files and setting the Project Instruction, run this prompt ONCE to activate the system. Claude will read every file, confirm it understands the system, and tell you exactly what it is ready to produce.
Step 3: The 3 Weekly Operating Prompts
After the activation prompt, these are the only 3 prompts you need every week. Run them in order on Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday.
BONUS: The Monday Analytics Prompt (Run Before Prompt 1 Each Week)

SOP_1_Channel_Strategy.docx.content:
1.1 The Channel Concept
This channel uses an AI-generated surgeon avatar — a 40-50 year old male, experienced-looking, in full surgical/medical uniform — to deliver elder health content in the most credible, click-worthy format proven in this niche. The channel is modeled after the two highest-performing reference channels in the space and built to outperform them systematically.
1.2 Avatar Specification
1.3 Target Audience
2.1 The 3 Ingredients of Every Viral Video
Analysis of 109 competitor videos reveals that every video exceeding 500K views contains all three of these ingredients. Missing even one drops average views by 60%.
2.2 Video Length Strategy
Target video length: 15 minutes. This is the optimal length for this niche based on channel analysis.
2.3 The 8 Content Pillars and Weekly Rotation
Each week, rotate through these pillars. Never publish two consecutive videos on the same pillar. This prevents the algorithm from narrowing your audience.
3.1 Publishing Schedule
3.2 Phase-by-Phase Plan
3.3 The 90-Day Content Calendar
Use this framework to populate your full calendar. Rotate pillars to prevent algorithm narrowing. Each row = one week.
4.1 The 6-Day Production Cycle
5.1 Core KPIs to Track
5.2 Milestone Tracker
6.1 Tone of Voice Rules
6.2 What Claude Must Know When Using This Project
Upload this as the pinned instruction in your Claude Project:

SOP_2_ShortForm_Script.docx.content:
1.1 Why Shorts Need a Different Formula
A Short is NOT a clipped version of your long-form video. It is a completely different psychological product. Your long-form video sells the viewer on spending 15 minutes with you. A Short must sell them on one thing only: clicking Subscribe and coming back for more.
2.1 The 3-Act Formula (60 Seconds)
Every Short has exactly 3 acts. Time allocations are non-negotiable. The viewer decides whether to swipe away in the first 2 seconds — which means Act 1 is everything.
3.1 Short-Form Hook Archetypes
These 5 hook types are proven to stop the scroll for the 60+ audience on mobile. The hook must begin the very first word of the script — no lead-in, no title card, no intro music before the first spoken word.
4.1 The Master Short-Form Script Template
Use this template for every Short. Target: 130-160 words. Delivery pace: 150 words/minute = 52-64 seconds. Leave 3-5 seconds of visual breathing room after final word.
5.1 How to Extract a Short from a Long-Form Video
Every long-form video should produce exactly ONE Short. Extract the single most shocking item from the list. Do not extract the solution — only the problem/revelation. The full solution is in the long-form video.
5.2 Standalone Short Ideas (Not Extracted from Long-Form)
Standalone Shorts are original 60-second ideas — not tied to a specific long-form video. They are often more viral because they feel more spontaneous and direct. Use these 5 formats:
BODY TEST Short: 'Do this 10-second test right now. If your [body part] does [specific thing], watch my full video tonight.' — Drives immediate action and subscribe.
SINGLE FACT Short: One shocking medical fact stated and explained in 60 seconds. No list. No multiple items. Pure single revelation.
COMMON MISTAKE Short: 'Every time you [common daily action], you are doing it wrong. Here is the right way in 60 seconds.'
TIMING Short: '[Common thing] at [specific time of day] is [shocking consequence]. The time matters more than you think.'
AGE PIVOT Short: 'This is fine at 40. After 60, it is dangerous. Here is why your body changes this process completely.'
6.1 On-Screen Text Rules for the 60+ Audience
The 60+ audience on mobile needs larger, simpler text than younger demographics. Every Short must have on-screen text reinforcing the spoken words. This also improves accessibility and watch time.
7.1 Full Example: Extracted Short from '9 Medications That Cause Rapid Dementia'
8.1 Pre-Production Short Checklist

SOP_3_AI_Avatar_Production.docx.content:
1.1 Avatar Visual Specification
The avatar is the single most important factor in building trust and CTR in this niche. The Dr. Adewale and Dr. Michael Kent channels both use a professionally credible doctor/surgeon appearance. This SOP defines the exact appearance parameters.
1.2 Costume and Setting Specification
2.1 The Standard Production Brief
Complete this brief for every video before submitting to your AI avatar generation tool. This brief tells the tool everything it needs to render consistent, high-quality output.
3.1 Script Section-by-Section Delivery Notes
Use these delivery notes when prompting your avatar tool or when writing direction notes in the script. Each section of the script requires a different emotional register.
3.2 Pacing Guide for 15-Minute Long-Form Videos
4.1 Script Markup System
Before submitting any script to your avatar tool, mark it up using these annotations. These tell the tool exactly how to render each line. Use square brackets for all directions.
5.1 Pre-Approval Render Checklist

SOP_4_Thumbnail_System.docx.content:
1.1 The 3-Element Thumbnail Rule
Every high-performing thumbnail in this niche uses exactly 3 elements. Dr. Michael Kent's thumbnails (avg 200K+ views) and Dr. Adewale's thumbnails (avg 150K+ views) both follow this formula without exception. Any thumbnail missing one element underperforms by 40-60%.
2.1 Background Rules
2.2 Text Rules
2.3 Doctor Face Rules
3.1 The 12 Formulas (Ranked by CTR)
These formulas are extracted directly from the highest-CTR thumbnails across Dr. Kent and Dr. Adewale's channels. Use these as your starting templates.
4.1 The Thumbnail Brief (Fill Per Video)
4.2 A/B Testing Protocol
Every video must launch with 2 thumbnail versions. YouTube allows you to test thumbnails natively. Here is the exact protocol:
Create Version A (your primary choice based on the formula)
Create Version B (same formula, different text or doctor expression)
Upload both to YouTube Studio > Customization > Thumbnails > Test and Compare
After 1,000 impressions, check which version has higher CTR
Keep the winner permanently. Delete the loser from your record.
Log all A/B test results in your analytics sheet for pattern learning over time
5.1 Thumbnail Anti-Patterns

SOP_5_Title_SEO.docx.content:
1.1 The 6 Click Triggers for the 60+ Audience
The 60+ audience clicks on YouTube videos for fundamentally different psychological reasons than younger audiences. They are not looking for entertainment — they are looking for answers to fears they already have. Every title must activate at least 2 of these 6 triggers.
2.1 The Master Title Formulas
Every title must be: under 70 characters (to show fully on mobile), contain an age group marker, contain a number or specific claim, and trigger at least 2 psychological click triggers.
3.1 The 5-Part Description Template
The first 2 lines of the description appear in YouTube search results before the viewer clicks. These 2 lines must function as a second hook — they must make the viewer click even if the title alone does not close it.
4.1 The 3-Tier Tag System
YouTube uses tags to understand video context and connect it to related videos. Use exactly 15-20 tags per video, structured in 3 tiers. Total character limit: 500 characters.
4.2 Tag Templates by Pillar
5.1 End Screen System (Last 20 Seconds)
Every video must have 3 elements in the end screen: 2 suggested videos + 1 subscribe button. End screens add an average of 8-12% additional watch time for elder health channels.
5.2 Info Cards System
6.1 Per-Video SEO Brief

SOP_6_Upload_Publishing_Checklist_and_SO.content:
Why the First 72 Hours Determine Everything
YouTube's algorithm makes a binary decision in the first 72 hours: is this video worth pushing to a wider audience, or is it not? Every action in this SOP is designed to give the algorithm the signals it needs to choose YES. Missing even 2 of these steps can reduce a video's reach by 40-60% permanently.
The Complete Upload Checklist
STAGE 1: Pre-Upload Verification (Day Before Upload)
STAGE 2: YouTube Studio Upload Settings
STAGE 3: First 24 Hours — Engagement Acceleration
These actions in the first 24 hours directly boost the algorithm signals YouTube needs to push the video further.
STAGE 4: Hours 24-72 — Monitoring and Response
Why Analytics Drive Everything
Every channel that stalls does so because the creator stopped listening to data. The analytics review is not optional — it is what tells you which pillar to double down on, which title formula is working, which thumbnail style is winning, and which item in the list is causing people to leave. Without this, you are guessing.
The Monday Morning 30-Minute Review Protocol
Core KPI Benchmarks for Elder Health
The Underperforming Video Rescue Protocol
If a video has been live for 7 days and is below target CTR — use this exact rescue sequence before abandoning it.
Day 1: Change thumbnail to the opposite formula (if you used dark background, switch to red. If pointing, switch to concerned face.)
Day 2: Update the title. Use a different formula from the 8 in SOP 5. Add a number if it did not have one. Add age group if missing.
Day 3: Add a pinned comment with a question that hooks viewers into the comments section.
Day 5: Share a Short extracted from this video to drive traffic directly to it.
Day 7: If still below target after all interventions, note the pillar and title formula as 'tested and failed' in your analytics log. Do not repeat this angle.

SOP5_AI_Avatar_Production_Brief.docx.content:
Section 1: Avatar Character Specification
Your AI avatar is the face of your entire channel. The design must be deliberate, consistent across every video, and engineered to signal credibility to the 60+ audience in the first 2 seconds. These are the exact specifications derived from analyzing the two highest-performing channels in this niche.
1.1 The Reference Channel Analysis
1.2 Your Avatar Specifications
1.3 The Two Avatar Modes
Your avatar should be generated in two consistent modes. Use the correct mode for each video type.
Section 2: Avatar Generation Prompts
These are the exact prompts to use in your AI avatar generation tool (HeyGen, D-ID, Synthesia, or similar). Run the base prompt first to generate your character, then use the mode-specific prompts for each video.
2.1 Base Character Creation Prompt
2.2 Mode A: Surgeon Mode Prompt
2.3 Mode B: Specialist Mode Prompt
2.4 Video Recording Direction Notes
When generating the video (not just still image), these are the direction notes that go into the avatar tool's prompt or director notes field.
Section 3: Script-to-Avatar Markup Guide
Every script must be marked up with delivery instructions before it goes to avatar production. These markers tell the avatar tool exactly how to deliver each line. Use this system on every script.
3.1 The Marker System
3.2 Marked-Up Script Example
Section 4: Pre-Approval Quality Checklist
Before any rendered avatar video moves to the editing or upload stage, it must pass every item on this checklist. One person approves every render against this list. Never skip items.
4.1 Avatar Visual Checklist
Avatar looks 40-50 years old — not younger, not older
Surgical scrubs OR doctor coat visible — correct mode for topic
Stethoscope clearly visible in every shot
Background is blurred medical setting — not a home or office
Eye contact is direct to camera throughout
Facial expression matches the content mood (urgent for warnings, empathetic for patient stories)
No unnatural artifacts or glitches in face or hands
Lighting is professional and clinical — not warm or lifestyle
Lip sync is accurate — no visible mismatch greater than 0.3 seconds
4.2 Audio Delivery Checklist
Pacing is 140-150 wpm baseline — not rushed, not too slow
[SLOW] markers are honored — key sentences delivered at reduced pace
[PAUSE] markers result in actual silence of correct duration
[URGENT] sections have appropriate vocal urgency — not monotone
Patient story sections have softer, more empathetic tone
Volume is consistent throughout — no sudden drops or spikes
No robotic intonation on technical terms — these should sound natural
The hook (first 15 seconds) has the highest energy of the entire video
4.3 Technical Checklist
Resolution: minimum 1080p, preferred 4K for long-form
Aspect ratio: 16:9 for long-form, 9:16 for Shorts
Frame rate: 30fps minimum
File format: MP4, H.264 codec for upload compatibility
Duration: 14-16 minutes for long-form (target 15 minutes as anchor)
Duration: 45-60 seconds for Shorts
No watermarks from the avatar tool visible in final render
Audio: 48kHz, stereo, -14 LUFS normalized for YouTube
4.4 B-Roll and Visual Enhancement Notes
The avatar render is the base layer. Add these visual enhancements during editing before upload.
Section 5: File Naming and Asset Management
Consistent file naming prevents confusion when managing 60+ videos per 90 days.

SOP6_Thumbnail_System copy.docx.content:
Section 1: The Psychology of the Elder Health Click
Your thumbnail has 0.3 seconds to stop a scroll. For the 60+ audience on YouTube, this happens differently than for younger audiences. Understanding the exact psychological triggers that cause this audience to click is the foundation of every thumbnail you create.
1.1 The 3 Click Triggers for the 60+ Audience
1.2 Channel Analysis: What Their Thumbnails Prove
Section 2: The 3-Zone Thumbnail Layout
Every thumbnail is divided into 3 zones. Each zone has a specific job. Follow this layout for every single video. Never deviate from the zone structure. The only variable is content within each zone.
2.1 The 3 Zones
2.2 The 4 Proven Layout Templates
Use one of these four layouts for every thumbnail. All four are proven from the reference channels.
LAYOUT A: Doctor Left, Text Right (Most Common — Use 50% of videos)
LAYOUT B: Doctor Right, Text Left (Use 25% of videos)
LAYOUT C: Full Doctor, Text Overlay (Use 15% of videos — for viral candidates)
LAYOUT D: Split Panel (Use 10% of videos — for comparison content)
Left panel: The danger (red-tinted, X mark). Right panel: The solution (green-tinted, checkmark). Doctor in center bridging both panels. Best for 'Stop this / Do this instead' videos.
Section 3: Thumbnail Text Rules
3.1 The 6 Proven Text Formulas
3.2 Typography Non-Negotiables
3.3 The Color-Psychology Pairing System
Every color combination must be chosen based on the psychological response needed for that specific video. Never pick colors for aesthetic reasons.
Section 4: Thumbnail Prop and Visual System
4.1 The Prop Library — One per Thumbnail
Every thumbnail should contain ONE prop element that visually represents the danger or solution. This gives the viewer a second visual story alongside the text and doctor. The prop must be recognizable at thumbnail size.
4.2 The Arrow System
Red arrows are one of the highest-CTR visual elements in elder health thumbnails. Both reference channels use them extensively. Here is the exact system.
ALWAYS use thick, chunky arrows — minimum 8-10px stroke weight. Thin arrows disappear at small size.
Arrow color: Bright red (#FF0000) or bright yellow (#FFD700) — never white or gray
Arrow points FROM the doctor TO the prop or text element
Arrow points FROM the text TO the most dangerous/exciting element
Maximum 2 arrows per thumbnail. More than 2 creates visual chaos.
Arrow should be at approximately 30-45 degree angle — diagonal feels more urgent than horizontal
Add a small circle or glow at the tip of the arrow to draw the eye
Section 5: Thumbnail A/B Testing Protocol
YouTube allows you to test multiple thumbnails. For every video, create 2 thumbnail variations (A and B). Run them in rotation and pick the winner at 48 hours based on CTR data. This single habit compounds across 60 videos into a massive CTR advantage.
5.1 What to Test
5.2 The Winner Criteria
5.3 The Thumbnail Review Checklist
Every thumbnail must pass this checklist before uploading. No exceptions.
Doctor is visible and identifiable in surgical scrubs or doctor coat
Stethoscope is visible in the image
Doctor's facial expression is clearly readable at 2-inch phone size
Text is ALL CAPS, maximum 4 words per line, maximum 4 lines
Text has black outline/stroke and is readable against background
Primary text color is either yellow, white, or orange — not gray or pastel
Background color matches the emotion needed (red = fear, navy = authority)
A/B version 2 has been created with one different variable
Both thumbnails tested at actual YouTube thumbnail size (320x180px) on mobile screen
No faces other than the avatar doctor appear in the thumbnail
No text in thumbnail is identical to video title — thumbnail text should TEASE, not repeat
File saved as: [PILLAR]-[VIDEO#]-THUMB-A.png and [PILLAR]-[VIDEO#]-THUMB-B.png
Image dimensions: 1280 x 720 pixels, JPG or PNG, under 2MB

SOP7_Title_YouTube_SEO copy.docx.content:
Section 1: The Science of the Elder Health Title
Your title does two completely different jobs simultaneously. Job 1: It tells YouTube's algorithm what your video is about so it can recommend it to the right people. Job 2: It triggers a psychological response in the 60+ viewer that makes clicking feel involuntary. Most channels only optimize for one. You must nail both.
1.1 How YouTube Ranks Elder Health Videos
1.2 The 8 Title Formulas (Proven in This Niche)
1.3 The Psychological Trigger Word Bank
These are the exact words that trigger involuntary clicking in the 60+ audience. Use at least TWO from the fear/authority bank in every title.
1.4 The Title Construction Rules
1.5 Real Title Examples (From Reference Channels)
Section 2: YouTube Description System
Your description is read by two audiences: YouTube's algorithm (which indexes every word for search and topic matching) and the rare viewer who scrolls below the video. Both must be served. The structure below optimizes for both simultaneously.
2.1 The 7-Part Description Template
Section 3: YouTube Tags Framework
Tags are less important than they were 5 years ago, but they still matter for initial topic categorization. Use the 3-tier system below for every video. Always use 15-20 tags total.
3.1 The 3-Tier Tag System
3.2 Tag Rules
First tag should always be your channel doctor name (Dr Adewale OR Dr Michael Kent Senior Health)
Second tag should always be the exact video title — this helps YouTube match your content
Include both singular and plural versions of key health terms (medication, medications)
Include both the condition AND the demographic: 'blood pressure' AND 'blood pressure over 60'
Never use competitor channel names as tags — YouTube penalizes this
Update tags if the video performs poorly in the first 2 weeks — add more long-tail terms
Section 4: The Video Metadata Template
Use this template for every single video. Fill in the bracketed fields. Do not skip any section. Completing this template before upload is the last step before the upload checklist.
Section 5: The First 72-Hour Optimization Plan
YouTube's algorithm makes its biggest decision about your video in the first 72 hours. It tests your video on a sample audience and measures CTR and watch time. If both are strong, it pushes to a wider audience. Here is exactly what to do in those 72 hours to maximize both.

SOP7_Title_YouTube_SEO.docx.content:
Section 1: The Science of the Elder Health Title
Your title does two completely different jobs simultaneously. Job 1: It tells YouTube's algorithm what your video is about so it can recommend it to the right people. Job 2: It triggers a psychological response in the 60+ viewer that makes clicking feel involuntary. Most channels only optimize for one. You must nail both.
1.1 How YouTube Ranks Elder Health Videos
1.2 The 8 Title Formulas (Proven in This Niche)
1.3 The Psychological Trigger Word Bank
These are the exact words that trigger involuntary clicking in the 60+ audience. Use at least TWO from the fear/authority bank in every title.
1.4 The Title Construction Rules
1.5 Real Title Examples (From Reference Channels)
Section 2: YouTube Description System
Your description is read by two audiences: YouTube's algorithm (which indexes every word for search and topic matching) and the rare viewer who scrolls below the video. Both must be served. The structure below optimizes for both simultaneously.
2.1 The 7-Part Description Template
Section 3: YouTube Tags Framework
Tags are less important than they were 5 years ago, but they still matter for initial topic categorization. Use the 3-tier system below for every video. Always use 15-20 tags total.
3.1 The 3-Tier Tag System
3.2 Tag Rules
First tag should always be your channel doctor name (Dr Adewale OR Dr Michael Kent Senior Health)
Second tag should always be the exact video title — this helps YouTube match your content
Include both singular and plural versions of key health terms (medication, medications)
Include both the condition AND the demographic: 'blood pressure' AND 'blood pressure over 60'
Never use competitor channel names as tags — YouTube penalizes this
Update tags if the video performs poorly in the first 2 weeks — add more long-tail terms
Section 4: The Video Metadata Template
Use this template for every single video. Fill in the bracketed fields. Do not skip any section. Completing this template before upload is the last step before the upload checklist.
